MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANTS JEFF SEIDMAN, DAVE FLORY AND CORT BUSINESS SERVICES CORPORATION'S, D/B/A CORT EVENT FURNISHINGS, DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: MELANIE POPPER, ESQUIRE (REVELATION LAW FIRM, 510.665.4195) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. LORRAINE OCHELTREE, ESQUIRE (DUANE MORRIS, 415.403.8834) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT. ON ITS FACE C.C.P. SECTION 428.10 ALLOWS FOR THE FILING OF A CROSS-COMPLAINT BY ADAMS. HOWEVER, THE CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED RAISES CLAIMS THAT ARE VIRTUALLY IDENTICAL WITH THE COMPLAINT AND NAMES TWO INDIVIDUALS. IN EFFECT, ADAMS HAS USED C.C.P. SECTION 428.10 TO AVOID FILING A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CROSS-C0MPLAINT IS SUBJECT TO DEMURRER. THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION ARE UNCERTAIN IN THAT IT IS IMPOSSIBLE TO TELL WHAT THE TERMS ARE OF THE ORAL CONTRACT AND WHICH CONTRACT OR CONTRACTS ARE BEING BREACHED. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS UNCERTAIN IN THAT IT FAILS TO ALLEGE WHAT EACH DEFENDANT DID AND WHEN. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
